# dynamoip

> Give local dev servers real domain names and trusted HTTPS — reachable from any device on your network.

dynamoip is a reverse proxy and DNS automation tool for local development and self-hosted services. It runs on your machine, registers DNS records pointing to your LAN IP, obtains or generates TLS certificates, and proxies HTTPS traffic to your local services by hostname.

## Two modes

**Pro mode** — uses a real domain you own, managed by Cloudflare. dynamoip sets A records in Cloudflare and obtains a Let's Encrypt wildcard certificate via DNS-01. Every device on the LAN trusts HTTPS out of the box.

**Quick mode** — no domain needed. Uses mDNS (`.local` hostnames) and mkcert. Other devices need the CA cert installed once.

## Installation

```bash
npm install --save-dev dynamoip   # recommended: dev dependency
npm install -g dynamoip           # or global
```

Requires Node.js >= 14. Runs on macOS, Linux, and Windows (Windows via Docker only).

## Config file: dynamoip.config.json

```json
{
  "baseDomain": "yourdomain.com",
  "domains": {
    "app": 3000,
    "api": 4000
  }
}
```

- `baseDomain` — domain root (Pro mode only). Can be apex or subdomain.
- `domains` — map of subdomain name to local port.
- `port` — proxy listen port (default: 443 with SSL, 80 without).

Omit `baseDomain` for Quick mode.

## Environment variables

| Variable       | Required            | Description |
|----------------|---------------------|-------------|
| `CF_API_TOKEN` | Pro mode            | Cloudflare API token with Zone:DNS:Edit permission |
| `CF_EMAIL`     | No                  | Email for Let's Encrypt expiry notifications |
| `LAN_IP`       | Docker on macOS/Win | Override LAN IP auto-detection. Set to your machine's LAN IP (e.g. `192.168.1.42`). Not needed on Linux. |

Place these in a `.env` file next to `dynamoip.config.json`. Never commit `.env`.

## CLI

```
dynamoip [options]

--config <path>   Config file path (default: ./dynamoip.config.json)
--port <n>        Override proxy port
--no-ssl          Plain HTTP only
--help            Show help
```

Always run via your package manager (not bare `sudo dynamoip`) so the binary is found in `node_modules/.bin`:

```bash
sudo npm run dev:proxy
sudo pnpm run dev:proxy
sudo yarn dev:proxy
```

## Running in Docker

Inside a Docker container on macOS/Windows, `os.networkInterfaces()` returns the container's virtual IP, not the host's LAN IP. `host.docker.internal` also returns the wrong IP (Docker Desktop VM bridge, not the LAN IP).

**Solution:** set `LAN_IP` to your machine's LAN IP before starting Docker. dynamoip checks `process.env.LAN_IP` first and skips auto-detection when it is set.

In docker-compose.yml:
```yaml
environment:
  LAN_IP: ${LAN_IP:-}
```

On Linux, use `network_mode: host` and leave `LAN_IP` unset — auto-detection works correctly.

See [docs/docker.md](docs/docker.md) for the full Docker guide including startup scripts for automatic IP detection.

## Cert cache

Certificates are stored in `~/.localmap/certs/`. Reused across restarts. Auto-renewed 30 days before expiry. Delete the directory to force a fresh certificate.

## Project layout

```
bin/dynamoip.js       Entry point — argument parsing, startup
src/config.js         Config loading and validation
src/cloudflare.js     Cloudflare API (DNS records, ACME TXT)
src/acme.js           Let's Encrypt DNS-01, cert cache, renewal
src/certs.js          mkcert integration (Quick mode)
src/proxy.js          HTTPS reverse proxy, WebSocket support
src/mdns.js           mDNS — dns-sd (macOS), avahi (Linux)
src/ip.js             LAN IP detection (env override + auto-detect)
src/cleanup.js        Signal handling, child process cleanup
docs/local-development.md   Using dynamoip as a dev dependency
docs/docker.md              Running dynamoip in Docker
```

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md) — full setup guide and reference
- [docs/local-development.md](docs/local-development.md) — installing as a dev dependency
- [docs/docker.md](docs/docker.md) — Docker support (macOS, Windows, Linux)
